response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong web and backend engineering background, but the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, deep-learning workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his resume. His exposure to low-latency systems (DLang at Sociomantic) and AI-adjacent tooling (MCP frameworks at re:cinq) is peripheral and does not bridge the gap to silicon-level performance architecture. The educational background (BS in CS, BS in Fine Arts/Photography) and career trajectory are firmly rooted in software product development, not hardware or AI accelerator engineering.
strengths: Long tenure and demonstrated breadth across embedded, systems, and web domains shows adaptability and systems-level thinking
Low-latency, high-throughput engineering experience at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases handling millions of req/s) indicates some performance-oriented mindset
Active open-source contributions and public technical talks demonstrate communication skills and community engagement
concerns: Zero evidence of SoC or IP modeling experience — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in deep neural network training, inference optimization, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator workloads
Principal/Senior IC level expectations (architecture feedback, hardware-software co-design, power-performance trade-offs) are well outside the candidate's demonstrated scope

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/DL workload or GPU performance expertise
Low-latency systems and performance-aware engineering background
